title: Pagination, Filtering, Links, Rate Limits, Versioning, and Resumable Work · Browser and Node Client Sys… · Compiler Case: Pagination Models
summary: An infinite-scroll accessibility view traverses cursor links, rate limits, filter encodings, resumable state, and focus restoration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts pagination models increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch resumable rate-aware API traversal. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Bound concurrent requests, queued work, connections, streams, memory, and downstream pressure from measured capacity. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
